fields.summary: Timeline service getEntities API should provide ability to get entities from given id
[Umbrella] Store, manage and serve per-framework application-timeline data

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description:
To support pagination, we need ability to get entities from a certain ID by providing a new param called {{fromid}}.

For example on a page of 10 jobs, our first call will be like
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&limit=11]
When user hits next, we would like to call
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&fromid=JID11&limit=11]
and continue on for further _Next_ clicks

On hitting back, we will make similar calls for previous items
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&fromid=JID1&limit=11]

{{fromid}} should be inclusive of the id given.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: The attached patch implements the fromId parameter.  I used camel case for fromId to match the other query parameters (primaryFilter, windowEnd, etc.).
Updated patch that adds fromTs and changes behavior when the entity specified in fromId is not found so that it returns no entities.

The _fromTs_ is needed because ATS can get events out of order. Without this parameter, a query with _fromId_ will return varying results depending on when you call it. The _fromTs_ prevents changes to the returned list so that it is consistent.

With the _fromTs_ param, the API calls would look like this

On a page of 10 jobs, our first call will be
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&limit=11]
When user hits next, we would like to call
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&fromId=JID11&fromTs=123456&limit=11]
and continue on for further _Next_ clicks
When the page is polling every 6s, it will keep calling the below so that data is consistent.
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&fromId=JID11&fromTs=123456&limit=11]

comments.body: The attached patch implements the fromId parameter.  I used camel case for fromId to match the other query parameters (primaryFilter, windowEnd, etc.).
Updated patch that adds fromTs and changes behavior when the entity specified in fromId is not found so that it returns no entities.

The _fromTs_ is needed because ATS can get events out of order. Without this parameter, a query with _fromId_ will return varying results depending on when you call it. The _fromTs_ prevents changes to the returned list so that it is consistent.

With the _fromTs_ param, the API calls would look like this

On a page of 10 jobs, our first call will be
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&limit=11]
When user hits next, we would like to call
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&fromId=JID11&fromTs=123456&limit=11]
and continue on for further _Next_ clicks
When the page is polling every 6s, it will keep calling the below so that data is consistent.
[http://server:8188/ws/v1/timeline/HIVE_QUERY_ID?fields=events,primaryfilters,otherinfo&fromId=JID11&fromTs=123456&limit=11]
